title: Run tests conditionally based on os_release

comments.body:
whops, looks like `platform.freedesktop_os_release` was added in `3.10` and we have to run on much earlier. @Honny1 I'd go back to the previous method. wdyt?

(edit)

I reverted the old behavior partially, we'll try to use `platform` when available and use a fallback code in other cases. the goal is to drop the fallback code in the future.

> I reverted the old behavior partially, we'll try to use `platform` when available and use a fallback code in other cases. the goal is to drop the fallback code in the future.

I think it's a good solution.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *Honny1*, *inknos*, *Luap99* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman-py). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman-py/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [Luap99,inknos] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

> I think this is the wrong approach long term though, you don't want to skip on the OS unless the feature is actually being dictated by the OS. In this case all these changes seem to be caused by the podman version so skipping on that seem most logical.

Fair point. To give context, in the prev team, I remember we used skipping on version in some tests, and it led to issues with testing coprs (or something, I don't remember exactly what was the issue, but it was annoying). so I am burned by the amount of failures I had to fix and in the end skip on distro (for fedora, centos, rhel) was better. So then, yeah, the solution is arbitrary and might not be optimal
